facts.text: Lima was renamed in 1808 to honor settlers from Old Lyme, Connecticut, and to avoid confusion with another New York town.
The Genesee Wesleyan Seminary, founded in 1822 in Lima village, was among the nation's first co‑educational schools.
Several historic farmhouses in Lima, including the Ganoung Cobblestone Farmhouse and the Leech‑Parker Farmhouse, are listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
North Bloomfield School, a 19th‑century building in Lima, was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1981.
Local groups such as the Quaere Club, Rotary, Masons, and the Chamber of Commerce have long supported Lima’s civic life.
